response.global_summary: The video begins with an intro sequence featuring driving footage from a game, followed by a live performance of the song 'Chiisana Koi no Uta' by a virtual performer. The final part of the video consists of a spoken MC segment by the virtual performer thanking the audience.

scenes.description: Introductory sequence with driving gameplay footage.
Virtual performer Rindo Chihaya sings 'Chiisana Koi no Uta' on stage.

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
In a vast world on a blue planet, one of just many in a vast universe
I send tiny little feelings of love to you on a tiny little island
I meet you, then some time goes by
I pour my heart into more and more letters
Before long we really resonate with each other
It’s intense at times between us, heartbreaking at others
My sweet song rings out far and wide, and changes the world

See? You’ve got somebody
Who means the world to you right here beside you
You’re the only one I hope this reaches. Let my love song ring

The moon shines down on us day after day
And on the dark path we’re walking down too
I feel so strongly about you
I promise you all eternity, never letting go of your hand in my grip

I bet you my feelings for you won’t change
And I’ll say the exact same thing when we’re on the brink of eternity
But it won’t be enough. Instead of tears, I’ll be filled with joy
And just give you a hug since I won’t be able to find the right words for it

See? You’ve got somebody
Who means the world to you right here beside you
You’re the only one I hope this reaches. Let my love song ring

If this is all a dream, don’t wake me up. If this is all a dream, don’t wake me up

response.thoughts:
**Video Analysis & Contextualization**

Okay, so I need to break down this video file. My initial assessment is that it looks like a performance video. Let me start by identifying the key segments: From the start to 00:11 is an intro with a driving scene, no vocals there, got it. Then, from 00:11 all the way to 03:55, that's where the performance happens, it's a song by a VTuber. Finally, from 03:55 to the end is just the VTuber doing some talking or MCing, essentially closing out the video.

The song seems really familiar, almost definitely "Chiisana Koi no Uta" by MONGOL800. I need to be sure. The performer is Rindo Chihaya, a VTuber, #輪堂千速. I'll make sure to note that properly.

Next, I'll define these segments as distinct scenes. Let's make it clean and simple: Scene 1 is the intro (00:00 - 00:11), Scene 2 is the song performance (00:11 - 03:55), and Scene 3 is the MC (03:55 - End).

Now, let's look for lyrics. I'll need to search for the lyrics to "Chiisana Koi no Uta" by MONGOL800. I'll start with the original Japanese lyrics, so I'll search "小さな恋のうた MONGOL800 歌詞." Then, I'll need to find them in English. So, "Chiisana Koi no Uta MONGOL800 lyrics English."

Finally, I'll wait for the search results, and make sure that it's all nicely formatted as JSON, ready to go.



**Finalizing the "Chiisana Koi no Uta" JSON Response**

Okay, I've got the lyrics and translation for "Chiisana Koi no Uta" by MONGOL800 gathered. Now, it's time to build the JSON response. I need to make sure everything is clean and accurate for the user.

First, I've mapped out the scene boundaries based on the information provided. The intro is a straightforward non-vocal segment from 00:00 to 00:11. The core of the song, "Chiisana Koi no Uta," runs from 00:11 to 03:55. After that, we have the talking/MC portion that goes from 03:55 until the end.

Crucially, when compiling the lyrics, I'm going to lean on the "lyrical-nonsense" source. It's known for its reliability, and I want to ensure the user receives the most complete and accurate lyrics available. I'll omit the MC segment; transcribing that isn't really the focus here.

Finally, a quick review to double-check those scene boundaries and ensure all the content is in place. Time to make sure this JSON response is spot-on for the user.
